title: azure-kv accepts credential=chained explicitly but module docs say 'absent (default)'
description: What is awkward: the match arm None | Some("chained") => CredentialMode::Chained makes credential=chained an explicitly accepted value, but the module-level doc comment says '- absent (default) — chain: managed identity first, then developer tools'. It does not mention that ?credential=chained is also valid. A user or contributor who reads the code finds an accepted value that the docs say doesn't exist. Suggested rewrite: either (a) add 'chained' to the URI format table in the module doc as a valid explicit value (preferred — it makes the API more predictable), or (b) remove Some("chained") from the match arm and make absent-only the canonical way to get the chain (simpler).
